title: NOC 41302 – Religious Leaders | GTR Immigration
description: Religious Leaders under NOC 41302 are spiritual and community professionals who provide religious services, moral guidance, and leadership within faith-based organizations across Canada. Classified as TEER 1 (Professional Occupation), this role supports Canada’s diverse religious communities and multicultural society. Religious Leaders serve in churches, mosques, temples, synagogues, and other places of worship, as well as in hospitals, correctional facilities, schools, and community institutions.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group41 – Professional occupations in law, education, social, community and government servicesSub-Major Group413 – Professional occupations in social and community servicesMinor Group4130 – Social and community service professionalsNOC Code41302

duties: Religious Leaders perform some or all of the following duties:
Administer rites such as marriages, funerals, baptisms, or other ceremonies
Deliver sermons and promote spiritual teachings
Supervise and administer religious education programs
Participate in humanitarian and community outreach activities
Assist in administrative and financial operations of religious institutions
Provide consultation services to government and organizations
Offer pastoral care in hospitals, schools, and correctional facilities
employment_requirements: Completion of college, university, or religious studies program usually required
Master of Divinity or Theology may be required for certain denominations
Period of study or mentorship with senior religious leader typically required
Recognition or ordination by religious authority required
Credential recognition may vary depending on religious denomination and province.
salary_prospects: $28,900 – $76,000 per year, depending on province, religious institution size, and seniority.
Additional housing or community benefits may be provided in some cases.
job_outlook: The outlook for Religious Leaders remains stable due to:
Demand for chaplaincy and counseling services
Demand varies depending on denomination and region.
labour_market_demand: Opportunities are spread across provinces, with larger urban centers showing higher demand due to diverse populations.
Religious Leaders often play a dual role in spiritual guidance and social services, strengthening their value within communities.
pathways: An official job offer from a recognized religious organization is often required.
